title: @majeanson/lac — The CLI
problem: Developers need a terminal-first way to create, search, validate, and manage feature.json files without leaving their workflow. Without a CLI, the only entry point to the LAC ecosystem is the editor or web app — both of which require more setup than a simple npm install.
analysis: The public entry point to the LAC ecosystem. Ships as a single npm package (@majeanson/lac) but bundles three binaries: `lac` (the CLI), `lac-lsp` (language server), and `lac-mcp` (MCP server). 16+ commands cover the full lifecycle — init, create, search, blame, lineage, stat, lint, spawn, archive, diff, export, and serve. Built on Commander.js with Zod validation from feature-schema. All commands operate on the local filesystem — no server required.
implementation: Entry point is src/index.ts, a Commander.js router that delegates to src/commands/*.ts. The filesystem walker (src/lib/walker.ts) and scanner (src/lib/scanner.ts) power all read commands by walking the repo for feature.json files. Config is loaded from .lac/config.json via src/lib/config.ts. The `lac fill` command delegates to lac-claude. Interactive commands use the `prompts` library for readline-based input. Static site export (lac export) generates self-contained HTML/CSS from src/templates/. Bundled with tsdown to ESM.

decisions.decision: Bundle all three binaries (CLI, LSP, MCP) into one npm package
decisions.rationale: Simpler install story — one `npm install -g @majeanson/lac` gives the developer everything. No need to separately install server components.
alternativesConsidered: Separate packages per binary — cleaner separation but friction-heavy install story for new users

decisions.rationale: Mature, well-documented, supports nested subcommands cleanly. The 16+ commands benefit from its structure.
alternativesConsidered: Yargs — more config, less ergonomic for deep command trees

decisions.rationale: Features live in the repo alongside code. Git tracks history. No infra to provision or maintain.
alternativesConsidered: SQLite sidecar — faster queries but severs the git-native workflow

knownLimitations: Bundling all three binaries means users who only need the CLI always install the LSP and MCP server too
Interactive prompts require a real TTY — lac fill and lac init don't work in headless CI without --yes flags
No concurrent write safety — two processes running lac fill on the same feature.json simultaneously will produce conflicts
File-walking is repeated on every invocation with no caching layer
successCriteria: `npm install -g @majeanson/lac` succeeds and `lac init` creates a valid .lac/ workspace. All commands run without error on a repo containing valid feature.json files.

lineage.spawnReason: The ecosystem overview needs a dedicated feature per package; the CLI is the primary user-facing entry point.
